entry.title: Embedded Analytics Architecture and Integration
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics Architecture and Integration: Part 1

 

SAP S/4 HANA  Embedded Analytics architecture comprises of 4 layers ,namely

  1. Persistency layer
  2. Modeling layer
  3. Access layer
  4. Visualization layer

In this blog I will be emphasizing more on persistency and modeling layer.

This section explains the building blocks that are part of the business reporting architecture and technical architecture. Building blocks such as persistency layer, modeling layer , access layer, and visualization layer are part of the business reporting architecture and building blocks : SAP S/4HANA backend system ,SAP S/4HANA frontend HTML5,and SAP Fiori frontend server.

Business Reporting Architecture

Let’s have a look at the business reporting architecture with the SAP S/4HANA

Embedded analytics and SAP FIORI UI. There are main 4 layers in SAP S/4HANA:

  • Persistency layer :  This layer refers to all the database tables of SAP S/4HANA . HANA is  the   native in memory database used to store  all the application tables in SAP S/4HANA for online analytical processing (OLAP) and online transaction processing(OLTP).
  • Modelling layer: This layer refers to all the ABAP core data services (CDS) views that are built on the top of the database tables. This tables in S/4HANA are exposed as virtual data models(VDMs) . VDM is intended to be interpreted by view browsers and other functionality which is based on the virtual data model.so, these views can be consumed directly by rich client UIs(such as Fiori tile, SAP Business objects )without any additional software layer.

 Before moving to VDM view types and Data Category we need to know about   Annotations . which is very much important in embedded analytics.

Annotation : which defines the technical and semantic attributes of a CDS object. It provides metadata for a filed/view which is understandable by different run time frameworks eg. Analytic Framework. Annotation can be further categorized into

  • View Annotations :For the entire CDS view ,placed in front of the DEFINE VIEW statement.
  • Element Annotations: For elements in the projection list ,placed in front of or after the element.
  • Parameters Annotation: for parameterized CDS view , placed in front of or after the parameter.
  • Extension Annotations: for CDS view extension ,placed in front  of the extend view statement.

 

Some of the important annotations :

@Abapcatalog.sqlViewName:’XXX’ :Auto creates SQL view corresponding to CDS view with the given name ‘XXXX’

@Abapcatalog.preserveKey:true :preserve key true ignores defaults keys from the database table/view and preserves keys defined explicitly in the view. possible values –true /false

@Accesscontrol.authorizationCheck:#check: Tells whether an authorization check should be performed or not.

@EndUserText.label:’xxxx’:It’s a free text which provides info to the end user.Maximum length would be 40 characters.

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter:true: Defines the  evaluation of filter conditions in path expressions of the CDS  view.

Furthermore few annotations will be discussed under VDM view types and Data category.

VDM view types and Data Category  :

                  

  • Basic View :  Basic view is created on top of the HANA database

@VDM.viewType: #BASIC

  • Composite View :  on top of basic view we will create a composite view. Here the source of the composite view is Basic view. Composite view cannot access directly from the database table.

@VDM.viewType: #COMPOSITE

  • Consumption View :  consumption view is the final view which is created on top of the composite view. This consumption view is accessed by UI Applications and other tools.

         @VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

  Data Category : which is going to define the data based on the business requirement.  Data Category has 3 types

  • Dimension :   otherwise called as basic view which represents the master data  e.x :  Material Master ,customer Master etc

@analytics.dataCategory:#DIMENSION

  • Fact : Represents Transaction data which contains Measures.

Should not be joined with master data views.

@analytics.dataCategory:#FACT

  • Cube: cube is also nothing but a transaction data but it can be mapped with Master data. Also contains measures. consumption views are built on top of the cube.

@analytics.dataCategory:#CUBE

Associations and joins are the two main topics to be known in embedded analytics.

Associations:

Associations are having similar functionality of joins to fetch data from multiple tables based on join condition ,but they are ‘joins on-demand’ which means they will only be triggered when user would access the required data which needs the association of required tables.

Associations are defined with ‘cardinality’.syntax:association[<cardinality>]

  • 0..1
  • 0..n or 0..*
  • 1..0
  • 1..n or 1..*

n should be an integer(non-zero),this basically establishes the relationship between the source and target table.

Here the source table is vbak and the target table is vbap which are associated together with cardinality 1..* which means ,for every one record in vbak with vbeln (on condition filed)there can be a minimum of 1 record and a maximum of  n(*) records in vbap with vbeln (on condition filed).

If we are confused about what kind of association, we should configure in the cds view then we can apply a rule of thumb: ‘always use: association [1]. This will always trigger an outer join and will work in all cases.

Joins:

It is used to join multiple table together to convert data into a meaningful information and represent to business.

Unlike the association ,if we have used 4 tables with join condition in cds view ,all the joins will get executed when we trigger cds view even though business is looking for fields from any two tables.

Supported join types:

  • Inner join
  • Left outer join
  • Right outer join
  • Cross join

 

 

 Note:  Look forward my next blog ,covering on access and   visualization layer.

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content: Hi Felipe,

entry.title: Physical samples per Handling Unit w/ EWM
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I need help in the following scenario:
1) Create PO -> inbound delivery -> Pack HU in EWM an post to inspection stock

2) I want to have the numbers of packed HUs in the No. Containers field in the inspection lot w/ corresponding UoM for sample item determination and sample size determination.

The goal is to have a 1:1 relationship between physical sample and HU so I can block one specific HU when a physical sample is rejected.

I now that there is this Help portal post: Quality Inspection for Handling Units | SAP Help Portal

But this scenario is not working because I use inspection type 1701.

Every help is welcome! Thank you

entry.title: Re: Automated email on PO Addition in SAP B1
entry.content: Thank for this, but still this involves a manual intervention, as the SAP B1 Mailer prompts to add the recipients. What we are looking here is, an email is auto sent, when a PO is added to Saved Distribution List

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
Hi Felipe, Thank you for the advice. I have an additional question based of your comment. If I were to make the freight cost on the landed cost document a standard amount and then post the actual AP Invoice with actual cost would that do the following:

1. Allow me to post a JE with a standard freight amount to a standard freight GL account?
2. There will likely be a variance between the A/P invoice and Landed Cost freight at standard. Can I route that variance to a GL account of my choosing?

entry.title: Re: First AI Project AI Core: Execution does not write Log
entry.content: Here is an update: I am finally able to see the logs. Putting it down here, if it may help someone... So after 30 mts of execution(last changed timestamp) I went to my "Workspaces" and selected the resourcegroup thereby setting the connection as the workspace context. After doing that I went to the ML Operations-->Executions and voila! was able to see the logs in the execution ID entry.

entry.title: Re: Guide: Deploying CAP Application with SQLite Database
entry.content:
From another response in this other thread about the same topic:

 
I had to add the standard sqlite3 dependency in the package.json:
"dependencies": {
"@sap/cds": "^7",
"@sap/xssec": "^3",
"@cap-js/sqlite": "^1.7.3",
"sqlite3": "^5",
"express": "^4"
},

entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content:
Hi @kaush_1 , yes, I've tried it multiple times and different dates, refreshing, reloading, restarting 🙂

I'm able to select the answers, but that error message is always there regardless; in addition, the submit answers button is always grayed out (inactive).

entry.title: Material not valid for plant in Manage material coverage app
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing the problem that when I want to check one specific material in the App Manage Material coverage, it says that the Material is not valid for that plant. When I go into Stocks/ Req. List I can see it for that plant correctly tho. I tried checking any differences in material master for this problematic material vs other materials that show for that plant but I couldnt find any. 

The exact error message it gives is this: Plant DE02 is not valid for material 9000037. The system has therefore changed the plant to DE01.

I already checked AoR so its not because of that. Anybody faced a similar problem?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Olá Comunidade,

Sou do Sul do Brasil, estou animada por fazer parte da comunidade, adoro compartilhar experiências e fazer novas descobertas.

entry.title: Re: Feedback gathering for GACE - person model for Goals
entry.content:
Hi @VanessaLi ,

Yes, this is a request we get from a lot of customers. Not only for GA/CE though; also for re-hires with new employment.

There are more reasons to want to have performance forms and goal plans on person level then on assignment level (same for Succession, Internal Recruitment, Compensation).

  • For global assignments you the home manager usually remains the end-responsible for the employee; you need the input from the host manager, but the home manager make the final decisions. Also as the global assignment is temporary so the home manager needs to approve the final performance rating as it can have financial consequences (bonus and/or salary increases) which goed from their budget.
  • For concurrent employment there can be a bit more discussion if the performance plans and performance goals should be on person or assignment level. Development goals always relate to the person and not the assignment. Some companies want to run the performance forms, performance goal plans and compensation cycles per employment and some want to combine them into one for the person.
  • For re-hires you want to be able to access the historical performance information without hassle (unless the performance forms are purged)

entry.title: Re: Automated email on PO Addition in SAP B1
entry.content:
The system will use email address automatically by priority as following:

1. email from contact person in PO

2. email from BP master data if there is no contact person of PO

If it still doesn't meet your requirement, you need to develop an addon for the specific case.

entry.title: Re: XSD Validation for DMEEX
entry.content:
Dear Richard,

Thank you for your blog post. Unfortunately, there is no error message in transaction FIBLFFP when the payment file is not valid. If you generate the payment file again in transaction FBPM, you see the error message in the log. But our end users in financial accounting don't use FBPM.

The expectation (or wish) is, that a failed XSD validation causes an abortion of the DME generation and a visible error message in transaction FIBLFFP and F110.

Facing this limitations, this XSD validation in DMEEX is not suitable for us.

Regards

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Multiple FU's are not created for the sales order
entry.content:
Hi Venkatesh, 

Since the dates (assuming delivery dates) are different for both materials, the created FU is probably created with multiple stages.

Does the FU have a stage from SHP1 to SHP2 as well?

Best Regards, 
Keerthana

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Web Intelligence to SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Has anyone had the issues described in note 3453821 and overcome them successfully? 

After upgrading our environment to 4.3 SP4 Patch 5 we're trying to connect to our Datasphere tenant and getting an "<STS> An unexpected error has occurred (FWM 02004)</STS>" error. Can't seem to find a way forward there, and I don't see many other people talking about the same problem.

entry.title: S/4HANA technical replacement for FK01 BDC Create Vendor with reference
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we are currently in a transition to S/4Hana 2023 FPS1.
I need to convert some programs and run into a little tool that calls FK01 to create a new vendor in another company code based on the same vendor from another company code via bdc.

What I found in the SAP Note that this can be done with the Transaction "BP" and then  “Create from Reference” (2579816).
However, I'm searching for a more technical ABAP based approach to replace the old BDC call of FK01 ideally by keeping (nearly) the same parameters.
I checked BAPI’s and other possible interfaces, but could not found a cunning replacement (e.g. checked Note 2484299)

Does someone solve this problem already?
Many thanks in advance.

KR
Markus

entry.title: Re: Overcoming Challenges in Staffing Customer Projects with SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Hi Suhasku, 

SAP Project and Resource Management covers a similar process as MRS RM, but it is a completely own solution built on BTP. For now SAP Project and Resource Management is only targeted towards staffing of human resource demands in projects. As you may know MRS can also plan and schedule other type of resources .

Best regards Renato

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q4-23 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Thanks @NicoWottke  for sharing this update

Under freight integration we can see the Sankey chart also includes transport footprints as well as footprints of each supplier. is it also possible to see this integrated footprint view per supplier, so we can compare the summation scope 3.1 and scope 3.4 emissions for each supplier? and if so where can we get this insight? This was a question of one of our customers.

Thanks in advance for your feedback 

#freighttransportintegration

entry.title: Re: Sloc to sloc against the bin to bin transfer
entry.content: We dont want change any destination any physical destination bin, Once the Physical desination bin is given will be the same still the TO confirmation. But in this case i could not find any field entry to enter the physical destination bin.

entry.title: Re: In CIDS How do you MERGE 2 INPUT files into 1 OUTPUT File
entry.content:
1. Create a field "Join_Field_1" in first table MARD and hardcode the value as '1'.

2. Create a field "Join_Field_2" in second table MSKA and hardcode the value as '2'.

3. Bring Row generation transform and provide the value as '1' in Row number starts at and '2' in Row count.

4. Join all 3 in one single transform.

5. Keep Row generation transform as a left table and perform left join with first table "MARD" and in the same transform again do left join with second table.

Bring all the fields from both the tables in the output. This will cause duplicate fields in the output.

6. And in the next transform to avoid duplicate fields do the concatenation of varchar fields and sum of decimal/int fields.

7. This way you will achieve the merge.

Thanks

Shubham

entry.title: Collective Billing of Solution Order:Include Prof.Serv. Project and Other Docs into single invoice
entry.content:
Co-authored by Klaus Kistner

Solution Business Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables you to sell bundled solutions combining products, services, subscriptions, long-term services, and projects to become a one-stopshop to solve complete customer problems [Ref : Selling Business Outcomes with Bundled Solutions].

As part of this scenario, it can make sense to create a single invoice per solution order.

In previous releases, it was not possible to converge billing data from customer projects (that are part of the same solution order) into a combined invoice with billing data from other documents that are also part of the solution order, such as service orders, service contract, and so on. [Ref: One Invoice Per Solution Order]. Customer projects had to be invoiced separately.

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, you can use billing type CBD2 to combine all billing-relevant follow-on documents of a solution order into a single invoice. Refer to note 3413050 for an example of how to create combined invoice of the solution order document.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to make SAP Ariba email notifications be treated as internal emails for our clien
entry.content:
Hi

We have a similar requirement and looking for solution on this. It looks like the Outlook Label showing External is managed by the buyer Outlook or the Email Engineering team, but to remove that External Label we were asked to get the Dedicated IP Address from SAP Ariba where the Emails from SAP would be sent and i think SAP team in the past mentioned that they cannot give a standard IP address range where the emails from SAP Ariba would be triggered.

If anyone was able to remove the External Label on the Ariba emails in outlook, please let us know how it was done and if there was any support or help you took from Vendor SAP in such case.

 

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Terms Of Usage - IDES ERP 6.0 incl. EHP8 on SAP HANA
entry.content:
Hi ALL,

Please, I need more details on Terms Of Usage of note 2432302 - IDES ERP 6.0 incl. EHP8 on SAP HANA :

Due to security reasons we strongly recommend that you do NOT install the IDES demo system in the same network as your productive environment

 

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: APIs for backup and restore of SAP HANA cloud database
entry.content: SAP HANA Cloud instances can be managed using a CLI. Additional details can be found at https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/create-and-manage-sap-hana-cloud-instances-using-cli.  The following tutorial group attempts to demonstrate using these CLI's and REST APIs for managing an SAP HANA Cloud instance.   https://developers.sap.com/group.sap-hana-cloud-automating.html.

entry.title: How to get started with Business Process Management?
entry.content:
Title:   How to get started with your BPM initiative.

Introduction

Hi everyone,  let me introduce myself by saying that I have over 30 years deep-seeded knowledge in the areas of Business Transformation and Enterprise Architecture.  I have been in this area as a Customer, a Partner to SAP and at SAP herself.  I would like to share some of my learnings and experiences with you.

You could read in my colleague Carolin’s latest blog post “Navigating Change with Business Process Management: A Strategic Imperative” what BPM is, etc.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/navigating-change-with-business-process-management-a-strategic-imperative/ba-p/13757044

I would like to continue from where Carolin left off with, how to get started with Business Process Management?  You will never find the perfect time to start, however we at SAP can support you.  SAP have been doing Business Process Management for decades and we continuously try to improve our processes.   In this blog I will address some of SAP best approaches and leverage what exists “there is no need to re-invent the wheel if it already exists”.

How to get started with BPM:

We mentioned earlier that BPM can be viewed as continuous improvement of various practices and initiatives or increasing automation to save costs.  Whatever your decision, there is more to continuous improvement than what you may realize. 

The first part is to look at this holistically, here I focus on three main disciplines which are: governance, method and tooling.  Here is an example below:

In this picture above we can see that there are processes for each discipline itself.  I know it might look confusing, but it really is not.   When customers ask me about how to get started, I always try to leverage from my experience and tell them to always start with Governance first and then follow up with a Process Management Lifecycle. The BPM Process Methodology can hold off in the beginning even though it is still relevant and should not be dismissed.   I also use these same topics when creating a BPM Process Office which I will address in a follow-up blog.

Let’s focus on the Governance part first.  If you follow a strong governance and stick to a given framework that works, then this will bring good results down the road.  Let me break down this topic a bit more.

Within this discipline we focus on the following:

Aligning your BPM Initiative to your company's strategic goals

Your BPM initiative must align to the strategic goals of the organization. First you need to understand the organization's strategic objectives and key performance indicators (KPIs).  Secondly, identify areas where process improvements can directly contribute to these objectives.  If you establish this link early on, you can realize that your BPM efforts, have a clear purpose and by doing so, you can see that you can deliver tangible business value.

Use a Framework & Structure as a baseline that works - Governance & Building Blocks as a foundation

We all need something to support us to get going with our BPM initiative, and many pre-defined frameworks can be used as a baseline.  If you have not defined a foundational set of building blocks as a framework, use what already exists and is available.   Here, we use these building blocks to establish governance structures, roles and responsibilities, and selecting BPM tools and technologies.  We also think about our Process Management Lifecycle and what is required to increase the level of automation across the cycle from analysis to design, modelling execution and monitoring.  

It's also important to mention that each building block has a level of maturity and requires consistent improvement.  It's not just about continuous improvement of the process.  I often use the BPM Building Blocks as a checklist to help me in phased approaches in achieving success out of various initiatives.  It brings good structure and practice.

Select a path or the building blocks that is right for your situation

Identify a path that is good for you.  There are so many ways to execute an initiative, which can involve multiple paths to realization.   You also need to consider;

  • what the maturity level is of your organization and whether or not the path you have chosen can be achievable within the timeframe
  • if there are any existing projects you can leverage before you decide on an approach.  An implementation project might be the right thing to focus on or any available project where there could be a small improvements eg. Operations, that you latch onto.
  • The amount of resourcing and skills that are available and whether these resources have time to support, do they require training and if so, what types of training is available to support them.
  • Any existing pain points and topics that are prioritized in the organization or something in a particular area of interest that are considered catalysts or North Star.  Usually these are tied into existing projects within the organization.

Think carefully before selecting the building blocks that are right for the situation at hand.  Again, as said earlier, it's not just about process improvement with the process management lifecycle it’s more.  But let’s start small, there is no need to “boil the ocean”.

Summary:

Once you have established a good governance and framework to work within you should be all set for a structured approach to any BPM related topic.  Ensure that you always align with the right key stakeholders and start your continuous improvement exercise within these parameters.

The core of BPM is organization (including people), process and technology.  However, for these three dimensions to connect and align is through, the process management lifecycle and to do this, we need good process leadership and management dealing with good governance and ownership.

I have two more blogs coming which is a continuation of this one, which ties nicely into the BPM Governance Building blocks.   On my next blog, I will focus on how to put these building blocks to good use that will bring value in a short term.  Also, stay tuned on how to set up a BPM Process Office, here I will address how to go about doing this in a  sustainable environment that will enable good practices and processes.  Stay tuned 😊

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to me here in the blog.

entry.title: Re: Simplifying Employee and Manager Experiences with Employee Central Quick Actions
entry.content:
Hi @ArjenVHooydonk,

Thank you for your suggestion regarding deep links. Currently, this feature is not supported. However, we greatly appreciate your feedback, as it helps us understand the demand for such enhancements. Your input is valuable, and we will certainly take it into consideration for future updates.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements List Report with Analytical Table - Performance
entry.content:
hi Kevin,

How did you set the threshold parameter in the manifest?

entry.title: Re: Simplifying Employee and Manager Experiences with Employee Central Quick Actions
entry.content:
Hi @anneliesdesme,

Thank you for your feedback, we appreciate your suggestions! We are pleased to inform you that the change marital status use case is already on our roadmap, and we double-check if the bank account can be supported as well. While we aren't able to provide a specific release date at the moment, please rest assured that it is planned for a future update.

Your input plays a crucial role in the process to improve the Employee Central Quick Actions, thanks again for your input.

entry.title: Re: BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 - Patch 2/3/4/5 Issues

entry.title: Left join merged dimensions
entry.content:
I don’t do merged dimensions much, but I have the mechanics working but I’m not getting a left join.

I have 744 records in Query A with 742 matches in Query B. But I want to see the blanks for the 2 missing records. Right now it’s only doing an INNER JOIN so only 742 display.

In table properties, I’ve checked “Show rows with empty dimension values”, but it’s not doing that.

=====
not sure how to tag this for Business Objects ... 🤔

entry.title: Re: Left join merged dimensions
entry.content:
still not working.

I created a detail under the merged dimension but that didn't totally work.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,  @David_Chaviano 

I'm excited about the digital skills initiative. I'm 28 years old female from Morocco. 

I believe it's an excellent opportunity for people like myself who work remotely or in underprivileged areas to have the same economic opportunities as those in more prosperous locations.

Additionally, I'm looking to switch careers, and this initiative could be a turning point. I'm currently learning about SAP's Cloud ERP solutions, which I think will be helpful in my future work.

Overall, I'm eager to continue developing my skills, and I think this initiative is the perfect way to do so. I hope there would be more in the future as there are no available opportunities in Morocco.

 

I fit all these categories :

  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
  • Women

#digitalskills #SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 


Best regards
Marwa

entry.title: Re: Task 7 - Using CQL in an unbound function implementation (July Developer Challenge - "Rever
entry.content:
Here my Submission.

cdsrepl is fantastic.

entry.title: SD Documents, Sales Documents, Sales Orders: Same Same, but Different?
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered why some people talk about SD documents, others about sales documents, and yet others about sales orders? Are these actually all the same documents, similar ones, or completely different ones? The answer is both yes and no.

All these documents belong to the same family, they’re all used in sales processes.

SD documents (short for sales and distribution documents) are the grandparents in this family:

SD documents comprise sales documents, billing process documents, and delivery documents. This means that sales documents are one of three children, to stick to the family analogy.

However, sales documents are also parents and have children of their own, the grandchildren of SD documents if you will. As with all children, they share some similarities, but each comes with their own characteristics. We’re of course talking about sales document categories here:

Every sales document category is broken down into sales document types. When you create a sales document, you have to pick a sales document type. For example, when you create a sales order, you select a specific sales order type: For standard processes, you create a standard order. When you sell a product from your consignment stock, you create a consignment issue. When you want to supplement the consignment stock, you create a consignment fill-up.

The sales document category and the sales document type determine what kind of sales document is created, which data you can enter in a sales document, which checks the system runs automatically, or which subsequent processes the system triggers. For example, when you create a sales order, the system determines prices for the products that you enter and checks their availability. For sales orders without charge, prices are not relevant, so the system automatically applies a discount of 100%. The products in a sales order also need to be delivered to your customer, so a delivery document would be a natural subsequent document. For a credit memo request, you wouldn't need a delivery document, but a credit memo as part of the billing process.

So you see, all these documents are very similar, but each one serves a distinct purpose.

The sales documents that you create are individual documents, but they are also one of several sequential process steps. Furthermore, you can create standalone sales documents, but you can also create a sales document with reference to another sales document, thus making the document chain shorter or longer. When you create a sales document with reference to another sales document, you can simply let the system copy data instead of having to enter it over and over again. The flow of data from one document to another also makes it easier to identify issues and solve them: The process flow (a graphical overview in SAP Fiori apps) or document flow (a list tree in classical applications) serves as a history that you can easily look up.

Like any good family reunion, we’ll end our little family story with a group picture:

 It shows well that while SD documents, sales documents, and sales orders are related, they’re not the same – just like in a real family.

 

PS: For those of you who are interested in a bit of family history, here are two interesting facts:

  1. SD (or sales and distribution) documents are a concept that goes back to SAP ERP and SAP R/3. In these earlier versions of SAP’s ERP software, Sales and Distribution (SD) was one of the most widely used modules. That’s why SD documents encompass sales documents, billing process documents, and delivery documents. In SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can find the sales process part of SD under Sales, while the delivery (or distribution) process part of SD is now part of Supply Chain. However, you can still find SD, for example, in the components used for tickets. Still all part of the same family!
  2. Similarly, sales orders without charge (also known as orders free of charge) used to be a sales order type in the past. However, the relationship didn’t fully reflect the characteristics of this document. It simply serves a different purpose than a standard sales order. That’s why in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, sales orders without charge are a standalone sales document category. From a twin, they changed into a sibling!

You can find extensive information about our sales document family in the documentation on the SAP Help Portal. Learn, for example, how sales documents are structured, more about the origin of data in sales documents, or how sales documents are controlled.

entry.title: Can't access fiori app after deployment
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm using CAP Node.js(odata v4), and have created a frontend using fiori elements. I am also using a Managed app Router. I was able to deploy it successfully to cloud foundry and it is showing in the HTML5 Applications tab in BTP, it was working fine before authentication was added. But after I added the authentication it gives a 403 forbidden error when I press go on the list report page.

 This is how my service file and xs security looks like:

I have also added the Admin role to the user and logged out of the account, and then signed in again so that the roles can be set but it still isn't working.  When Only the 'authenticated user' authentication in applied on the service or entity in service file it works fine, the problem occurs only I when use the @requires: 'Admin' authentication on the entity.

entry.title: Re: Deleting the status dependant project version
entry.content: How the issue is resolved? Can you please help

entry.title: Re: CXML file conversion in SAP PI
entry.content:
We had to do this and did by using a standard XSLT mapping 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:fo="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Format">
<xsl:output method="xml" doctype-system="http://xml.cXML.org/schemas/cXML/1.2.047/InvoiceDetail.dtd"/>
<xsl:template match="/">
<xsl:copy-of select="*"/>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

entry.title: Unable to generate customized template in SAP Enable Now Producer
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am relatively new to SAP Enable Now and all its components. This time, I have been testing different template customizations for documentation and I have been getting the following problem.

I just slightly modified, by adding an icon at the top right part of the slide, the PowerPoint template for documentation generation as shown in the following pictures:

But, whenever I generate a PowerPoint for an scenario, the template doesn't seem to generate:

Any thoughts on how to proceed in this scenario? I will be greatly thankful.

Regards,

Sebastián

#SAPEnableNow #Producer

entry.title: In table PA_VLE_ENROLL_SEAT there is a field ATTENDANCE_DURATION. need to know the measure of it.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have developed a plateau report(Custom LMS report )for retrieving the VLS attendance data by using two tables PA_VLE_ENROLL_SEAT, PA_VLE_EVENT. 

In table PA_VLE_ENROLL_SEAT there is a field ATTENDANCE_DURATION , I am looking for the measure of data that is being stored  .Whether it is in Minutes or Seconds or Hours or Attendance percentage.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error while creating the sale order in SAP HANA 1610
entry.content: Hi All .. This issue has been fixed by adding SSL Server standard certificate into SSL Client SSL Client (anonymous)

entry.title: Re: Error when calling Credit Management: Technical Error Message no. UKM_PI008
entry.content: Hi All .. This issue has been fixed by adding SSL Server standard certificate into SSL Client SSL Client (anonymous)

entry.title: How to can I integrate Configuration and Security Analysis in FRUN with SAP Analytic Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using SAP Focused Run 4.0 FPS02.
Configuration & Security analysis data are retrieved successfully on our system.
We would like to extract the data that are retrieved in CSA store to view in SAC.
How can we proceed ?

Thank you


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to can I integrate Configuration and Security Ana...
entry.content:
You can use the OCC Dashboard to pull in and check CSA data (this shows how to create views):

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-focused-run/expert-portal/occ-dashboard.html

Then this same data can be integrated with SAC via OCC queries (be sure to select CSA tab under heading "Specific Dimensions"):

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-focused-run/expert-portal/sap-analytics-cloud-integration.html

This blog is a good summarization:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-analytic-cloud-system-recommendation-from-solution-manager-and/ba-p/13495916

The part Data validation you have mostly already completed by configuring CSA (perhaps you still need to create views in OCC). From Data extraction it shows you how to pull this into SAC.

entry.title: SBPA Workflow Log retation period

Mauricio

entry.title: Re: Absence half day, leave request half day with start t...
entry.content:
Hi Volker_Ruof,

Thank you for your quick reply. I have already gone through that blog but this is not actually our need.

Our need is to use the clock times and not the duration  when requesting a half day. The struggling point is that there is no way to get the start time and end time of a break of the day model assigned to the start time of the absence in time off when an employee request an absence (using rules assigned to MDF object Employee Time , purpose Evaluate) .are there any improvement that could address that kind of need in the future ?

Another struggling point we faced is that there is no function to add hours to a start time  (field of type Time), example : start time = 08:00:00, ==> add 4 hours => target start time = 12:00:00

Rachid.

 

Rachid.

entry.title: Re: Implementation Tips - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil
entry.content:
Hi @VijayKonam ,

Sorry somehow I did not receive the notification of your question.

The DRC NF-e is a BTP solution which uses some components in S4. When you license our DRC NF-e BTP solution, you have the rights to use the functionalities delivered for Brazil in eDocument Cockpit.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Kyma environment ready for Production?

Did you do the tutorial on a fresh Kyma environment on a Trial account? If you have an older working environment, there may be things solved within it that are there either because of the Trial account or fresh environment.

entry.title: São Paulo - SAP Stammtisch 8.8.2024 18:00 BRT
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in São Paulo has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

SAP Stammtisch in São Paulo will happen on August 8th, 2024 at 18:00 BRT for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", which will help us to try and reserve the right number of places at our meeting point, the Tatu Bola Bar.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates South America Roadshow  

and we also invite you join the São Paulo Group if you are not yet the member:  SAP São Paulo
